entry.title: Re: Impairment Posting Error After Missing Fiscal Year Ba...
entry.content:
Thank you very much for your answer.

It appears that adding an interval and an additional depreciation key (ILI) caused a depreciation plan to be generated for 2024, which in turn prevented an impairment charge from being recorded at the end of 2025.
In other words, while “adding an interval and an additional depreciation key” is the correct procedure, the resulting “retroactive depreciation plan” means that “an impairment charge cannot be recorded at the end of fiscal year 2025 (because a plan was created in advance that would reduce the book value to zero).”

I believe that the sequence “adding an interval and a depreciation key (April 2026)” → “retroactive depreciation plan (April 2024)” is behavior not anticipated by SAP.

Best regards,
kanki

entry.title: Re: 📢 SAP Inside Track Chennai - June 2026
entry.content: Where to register?? Pls share registration link

entry.title: Re: D17388 Add 'Street2' in screen for selecting...
entry.content: Hi, it seems that same issue has been raised in SAP ticket. Please follow the suggestion from corresponding product support.

entry.title: Re: Safety Stock Alert
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

In SAP Business ByDesign, automatic alert or email for Safety Stock is not supported, and Safety Stock is monitored only through Supply Planning, this is system standard behavior, please refer to KBA "3736823 - No Automatic Alert or Email for When Safety Stock Level Breach".

As workaround, you can build a report based on the data source Supply Planning Product Exceptions and only display data if the exception type is "Safety Stock Violation". This report can be send or 'broadcasted' to Email recipients e.g. once a day (See Business Analytics work center, view Broadcast).

Henry

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Safety Stock Alert
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer as solution. You can un-accept it anytime if you have further questions.

entry.title: Joule for Developers (J4D) Not Visible in BTP Entitlements
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support Team,

We have already contacted our SAP Account Executive and completed the contract/signature process for SKU DIGITS related to Joule for Developers.

However, we still cannot see the J4D service under the Entitlements section of our SAP BTP Subaccount / Global Account.

Could you please help us understand:

  • Whether there is an additional activation step required from SAP side
  • If the entitlement needs to be manually assigned
  • Or if there are any prerequisites that are currently missing in our landscape

At the moment, we are unable to proceed with the activation because the service is not visible in BTP Entitlements.

Could you please check and guide us on the required next steps?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Joule for Developers (J4D) Not Visible in BTP Entitle...
entry.content:
Based on KBA 3567298 - Inability to View AI Unit Consumption in Business AI application - SAP for Me,

the Business AI application in SAP for Me will only display entries if your landscape is using SAP AI Unit SKU 8019164.

In addition, AI entitlements purchased or signed between October 5, 2023, and August 4, 2024 are not displayed on the SAP for Me Business AI card. These older purchased Units SKUs cannot be shown.

To proceed, please contact the appropriate team as outlined below:
- SAP Account Manager (per 2626344)
- SAP Order Management (per 1660069)
  - Email: sapcontractcenter@sap.com

entry.title: Re: Develop RAP-Based SAP Fiori Applications in Minutes with SAP Fiori Tools AI Project Accelerator
entry.content: @kunalkd please check if your abap cloud system version is 2602 or higher.

entry.title: Re: GL Account Synchronization finished successfully but...
entry.content: Thank you for your response. However, I would like to confirm one more point: how can I change the value of “G/L Account Not Used” to No? I have tried changing it by unchecking this option in the “Manage Operating G/L Accounts” app, but after saving, it reverts back to the original value. (In the “Manage G/L Accounts Master Data” app, I cannot find this field either.) Is there any other way to change this setting?

entry.title: Re: ABAP list based PI Sheets on S/4 Hana Cloud Private e...
entry.content: To meet this  requirement,  you can consider implementing your own logic with BAdI Workorder_update or enhancement PPCO0008

entry.title: Re: GL Account Synchronization finished successfully but...

I have tried changing it by unchecking this option in the “Manage Operating GL Accounts” app, but after saving, it reverts back to the original value. (In the “Manage GL Accounts Master Data” app, I cannot find this field either.) Is there any other way to change this setting?

entry.title: how can I change the value of “GL Account Not Used” to No
entry.content:
how can I change the value of “GL Account Not Used” to No?

I have tried changing it by unchecking this option in the “Manage Operating GL Accounts” app, but after saving, it reverts back to the original value. (In the “Manage GL Accounts Master Data” app, I cannot find this field either.)

Is there any other way to change this setting?

entry.title: Is It Okay to Use Only RECN Instead of ABST for Reconciliation Accounts in SAP Account Group?
entry.content:
In SAP FI, when configuring Account Groups for reconciliation account purposes, ABST (AP/AR) has traditionally been used. However, I find it more convenient to use RECN only.

Are there any known issues or pitfalls when using only RECN instead of ABST for reconciliation accounts? For example:

  • Impact on Special G/L Indicator processing

  • Restrictions when using Alternative Reconciliation Account

  • Integration issues between subledger (S/L) and general ledger (G/L)

  • Effects on standard reports or analytics tools

RECN is more convenient for me, but I'm concerned about potential hidden issues long-term. If you have practical experience or know of official documentation addressing this, please share.

Looking forward to meeting many of you in person!

Thanks,

Vinod. K

entry.title: Does the HCM Compatibility Pack expiry (May 31, 2026) apply to a Simple Finance 1503 (ERP 6.0 EhP7)?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

System: SAP Simple Finance, on-premise edition 1503 — technically SAP ERP 6.0 EhP7 (SAP_BASIS 740, per SAP Note 2117481), with classic ERP HCM on SAP_HR 604 / EA-HR.

My understanding is that the Compatibility Pack is an S4CORE-based SAP S/4HANA construct. Since Simple Finance 1503 is not S4CORE but part of SAP Business Suite 7, its HCM should follow Business Suite 7 maintenance (2027 / 2030) — not the May 31, 2026 Compatibility Pack expiry.

Questions:
1. Is it correct that the May 31, 2026 HCM Compatibility Pack expiry does NOT apply to a Simple Finance / S/4HANA Finance 1503 system?
2. Does the Compatibility Pack / H4S4_1 constraint only become relevant after conversion to S4CORE-based SAP S/4HANA (1511+)?
3. Is there an official SAP Note that explicitly distinguishes S/4HANA Finance (Business Suite based) from SAP S/4HANA (S4CORE) regarding the HCM Compatibility Pack?

Thanks for any official references.

entry.title: Supplier DPR creation We are facing error _Account 65001000 does not exist in chart of accounts YCOA
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

While attempting to create a supplier down payment request with a WBS element in the test system, we encountered the following error: "Account 65001000 does not exist in the chart of accounts YCOA."

We did not encounter this error during testing in the custom system. In the custom system, we observed that this GL account (65001000 - Purchase Service) was blocked in the master data, and its implementation status was set to 'In process'—however, these settings were not transferred to the test system.

My question is: What is the relationship between this GL code and the supplier down payment process? We did not configure this setup.

We do not want to use this GL code.

Please let me know if there is any standard SAP configuration related to this.

Thanks & Regards,

DNR

entry.title: Does the WBS field on the sales order have an extension point?
entry.content:
I need to write a custom field from material master data (model) to the WBS field on the sales order line item. However, when implementing this custom logic, I noticed that the WBS field is not available in the CHANGING parameters of the extension point I selected. Later, I tried other extension points but still couldn't find the WBS field. Which extension point should I use to achieve the logic described above? Or does the public cloud not support custom logic for the WBS field on sales orders?

Below are the extension points I have tried:

- Market Segment Enhancement Substitution: FINS_MARKET_SEGMENT_SUB
- Modification of Business Data in Sales Document Item: SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM_BUSINESS
- Data Transfer for Contract Item Assignment: SD_ASSG_CONTRITM_DATATRANSFER
- Item Change in Outbound EDI Sales Order/Return Order: EDI_SD_ORDER_CONF_OUT_ITEM

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM DODO — The 4 Variants You Must Know Before You Configure (DODO in Embedded S/4HANA 2025
entry.content: Great Blog Yogesh. Thanks for sharing those details!

entry.title: Re: Revision level for a material in S4 public cloud
entry.content:
Good day @Noddy_R 

Thank you for your question.

In Public Cloud, you cannot use completely free revision level values in the same way as ECC external revision levels if those values are not supported by the maintained revision level configuration.

Note that revision levels are determined from the Define Revision Levels for Materials configuration activity, and that you must enter a change number before the system allows you to create a revision level in Manage Product Master Data. KBA 3259773 provides further details.

So, in simple terms:

  • Removing all revision levels you configured will not allow users to enter any value freely.
  • You must maintain revision levels in the allowed sequence or definition in your configuration.
  • A revision level is normally assigned with reference to a change number.
  • If the required level, for example P1 to P5, is not part of the configured revision level logic, the system will not treat it as freely allowed external input.
  • This is a standard restriction in Public Cloud compared with the more flexible ECC setup.

 

Here is what I recommend:

  1. First, maintain the required revision level sequence in Define Revision Levels for Materials.
  2. Include values such as P1, P2, P3, P4, P5 if you require them for preproduction.
  3. Use a change number (in Manage Product Master Data) when you assign the revision level to the product.                   
  4. For mass migration, check whether the migration object or available API supports the revision level field.
  5. If the migration template/API does not support it, load the product first and then assign revision levels through the supported app or process.                                                                                                                

 

Compare the following references for more insights.

ompare the following reference for more insights. 3259773 - "Create Revision Level" button is greyed out in the app Manage Product Master Data - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

2853498 - Error 'Revision level cannot be created for material' appears when using the App 'Create Material Revision Level' - SAP for Me

 

To summarise:

Deleting all revision level configuration in Public Cloud will not make revision levels free text. Revision levels are controlled by the configured revision level definition and normally require a change number during assessment. If ECC used fully flexible external revision levels, you should adjust the Public Cloud design by maintaining the required allowed revision values, such as P1 to P5, in the standard configuration.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: SENARIO: OVER CONSUMPTION OF RAW MATERIAL IN SUBCONTRACTING
entry.content:
Scenario

PO (Subcontracting)

  • Finished Goods: 10 MTR

Components sent to vendor

  • Raw Material: 10 KG

IDEAL PLANNED FINISHED GOOD : 10  OUANTITY UNIT IS MTR . 

Actuals

  • Vendor delivered: 6 MTR FG
  • Vendor consumed your material: 8 KG
  • Vendor used his own material: 2 KG
  • Vendor is claiming reimbursement for 2 KG of raw material supplied by him

Now we need to create a Subsequent Debit note for extra 2 kg of raw material in reference through purchase order so that it shows in purchase order history because i need to create a Bapi for it . 

and i am getting problem while create Subsequent Debit note of raw material as it is picking finished good unit MTR in PO reference .

entry.title: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
entry.content:
The "effective" configuration for a given project is made up from values that can be taken from a number of different source locations, shown here:

There are built-in defaults, user-specific defaults, static and user-specific project settings and more. These can be specified in, and will be retrieved from, various files and environment variable values.

Regard this sample configuration section:

"cds": {
  "requires": {
    "db": "sql"
  }
}

This value of sql as the kind of database requires can also be specified on the command line of Unix and Unix-like systems (anything other than Windows, effectively) using an environment variable.

What is the name of that environment variable (hint: like most environment variables, it will be in ALL_CAPS)?

This is a question from the June Developer Challenge on CAP Knowledge. And don't forget: always submit your answer as a hash, on its own - read the Taking part section of the intro post for more info. At the end of today, this question will be updated with links to further reading on this topic.

entry.title: Re: We want to know where FU_CODE Follow up action code a...
entry.content: Chain ID can be found from table MSR_D_EXECUTED with DOC_NR = returns order number(10 digitals).

entry.title: Enhanced User List Performance for Document and Reporting Compliance Türkiye
entry.content:
Introduction
This blog post introduces a recent enhancement in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Türkiye in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. It has been designed to improve the performance and user experience of retrieving registered user list for the  eInvoice and eDelivery/eDespatch processes for customers whose service provider is SOVOS.

Background 
In Türkiye, if your business exchanges electronic invoices and electronic delivery notes, you must maintain an up-to-date list of registered users. This list lets you check whether your trading partner is officially registered with the tax authority as an electronic user. 

Traditionally, if you were using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Türkiye, you relied on two separate applications to retrieve this user list information: 

  • Retrieve Delivery Note Users
  • Retrieve eInvoice Users  

These applications populate the corresponding SSCUI configuration tables, which are consumed by the eInvoice and Delivery Note processes:

  • Default Partner Alias for eInvoicing (ID: 103088) 
  • Define Default Partner Alias for Delivery Notes (103104) 

Limitations of the existing approach: 

  • In the S/4 Public Cloud environment, unlike the Private Cloud, heap memory extension is not possible. 
  • Performance – As the number of registered users in Türkiye continues to grow, the existing solution is not optimized to efficiently process large user lists, which can result in longer execution times and reduced system performance. 

What's New? 
To address the limitations above, SAP has introduced an alternative approach for SOVOS customers to retrieve user lists. User list retrieval is now integrated into the standard Process eDocument Inbound Messages application, the same application that you already use for processing incoming e-documents as part of business operations. 

Key enhancements: 

  • Improved performance — Large user lists are now processed in chunks, enabling efficient retrieval and persistence even for high volumes of registered users. 
  • Unified User Experience – User list retrieval is performed through the same Inbound Message Handler application used for other inbound e-document processes, providing a consistent and streamlined experience. 
  • Automatic Submission and Persistence – The system automatically requests, receives, processes and persists in the retrieved user list without requiring any additional user action. 
  • Full Traceability – Technical eDocuments are created in the Manage Electronic Documents application, providing complete traceability through detailed application logs and a history tab that displays information such as the request file, file name, and correlation ID. 

Two new message types have been introduced to drive the user list pull through the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application: 

ProcessMessage Type
Delivery NoteTR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL 
eInvoiceTR_INB_USERLIST_PULL 

Prerequisites

  • This enhancement is available only for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 
  • As a customer, you must use SOVOS as the service provider for Türkiye Document and Reporting Compliance. 
  • Source type PULL needs to be activated for the company code in the relevant SSCUI. 
  • Customers should edit the respective communication arrangement of both eInvoice and eDespatch and deploy the same to get the new API changes. 

New Process to Retrieve the User List 
The user list retrieval process is now fully integrated into the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application. The following steps describe how to execute the process. Subsequent sections explain how to monitor the results using the Manage Electronic Documents, Application Log, and History tab. 

Running the Inbound Message Handler 

  1. Open the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application. 
  2.  Enter the Company Code. 
  3. Enter the Country/Region as Türkiye (TR). 
  4. Select one or both of the following actions: 
    • Get Available Messages – Specify the Received-On date to retrieve new messages from SOVOS. 
    • Process Existing Messages – Specify the Received-On date to process messages that have already been retrieved. 
    You can execute either action independently, run them sequentially (first Get Available Messages, then Process Existing Messages), or execute both actions in a single run. 
  5. Select the appropriate Message Type: 
    • TR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL – Retrieves eDelivery Note users. 
    • TR_INB_USERLIST_PULL – Retrieves eInvoice users. 

  6. Click Execute to start the process. 

The example below illustrates the configuration required to retrieve the eInvoice user list using the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application and the TR_INB_USERLIST_PULL message type. This is provided as an example only. The same configuration steps can be followed to retrieve the eDelivery Note user list by using the TR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL message type. 

Once the process is executed, the system: 

  • Creates technical eDocuments in the eDocument Cockpit. 
  • Automatically updates the SSCUIs Default Partner Alias for eInvoicing (ID: 103088) and Default Partner Alias for Delivery Notes (103104) with the latest user list data. 
  • Handle auto-submission of technical eDocuments, you do not need to take any further action. 

Scheduling the Job 
To ensure that the user list remains up to date, SAP recommends scheduling the process at a frequency that meets your business requirements. 

Create separate scheduled jobs for: 

  • eInvoice user list retrieval using message type TR_INB_USERLIST_PULL 
  • eDelivery Note user list retrieval using message type TR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL 

The scheduling frequency can be configured according to business needs. 

Monitoring in the eDocument Cockpit 
Once the message handler is executed, the corresponding eDocuments will be visible in the eDocument Cockpit. The two flows can be distinguished by the Type Description: 

ProcessType Description
User List Download Request (eInvoice) Türkiye Incoming Pull Req Inv 
User List Download Request (eDelivery Note) Türkiye Incoming Pull Req Deln 

These are technical eDocuments only. No business documents (such as invoices or delivery notes) are created. The eDocuments serve as tracking records and for audit purposes for the user list retrieval process.

Process Statuses 
The eDocument can have the following key process statuses: 

  • User List Processed — The user list pull and persistence completed successfully. 
  • User List Unprocessed — Something went wrong during the flow. The exact error can be reviewed in the Application Log. 

Application Log 
The Application Log provides a detailed overview of each execution run. For each processed chunk, the log displays the following information: 

  • Total number of users retrieved  
  • The number of users persisted (newly added). 
  • The number of users updated. 
  • Number of users deleted. 

History – Request File 
The history tab of the eDocument provides access to the request file associated with each retrieval run. The request file provides important technical metadata, including: 

  • Correlation ID (only specific to SOVOS) — Enables end-to-end tracing of a specific chunk throughout the processing flow. 
  • File name — Identifies the file received from the SOVOS service. 

Note: The file name is generated and provided by SOVOS. SAP does not control or modify the file name, the system stores the file name exactly as received from the service provider. 

Summary
With this enhancement, the user list retrieval processes in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Türkiye have been integrated into the standard Process eDocument Inbound Messages application.  As a result, customers using SOVOS as their service provider can use the Process eDocument Inbound Messages application as a primary process and keep the existing Retrieve eInvoice Users and Retrieve Delivery Note Users applications as an alternative. 

 Key takeaways: 

  • Use TR_INB_USERLIST_PULL for eInvoice and TR_DLN_USERLIST_PULL for Delivery Note. 
  • SSCUIs Define Default Partner Alias for eInvoicing (ID: 103088) and Define Default Partner Alias for Delivery Notes (103104) are populated automatically when the application is run. 
  • Schedule the report once per process based on your business requirements. 
  • Monitor processing through the technical eDocuments generated in the eDocument Cockpit, identified by their respective document type descriptions. 
  • Submission and processing are handled automatically by the system, eliminating the need for manual intervention. 

This change provides a more performant, transparent, and unified experience for SOVOS users managing their Türkiye compliance flows.

entry.title: Unable to find the Solution process flow diagram from CALM and also from SAPforMe
entry.content:
Unable to find the Solution Process flow diagram from CALM and also from SAPforMe for S/4HANA Private Cloud 

Below are the best practices not having the Solution Process Flow diagrams

2FM

2QY

BGC

2JB

1MN

31L

6JM

BKN

1O0

2YJ

1BS

43B

2QU

1JI

3FY

entry.title: Re: Incorrect quantity confirmation for sales order in AA...
entry.content:
PAL with sequence constraint means allocations are consumed in the defined customer sequence until the available quantity is exhausted. When you created sales orders for customers 17100003 → 17100004 → 17100005 → 17100006, the system checked each allocation bucket sequentially. Even though the total stock was 125 PC, the allocation sequence and remaining unconfirmed quantities from earlier customers restricted confirmation for later ones.

By the time you reached customer 17100006, the earlier allocations (10 + 55 + 40 = 105 PC) had already consumed most of the available stock. The system then applied sequence constraint logic, which prevents later customers from receiving full confirmation if earlier ones haven’t been fully satisfied or if the allocation bucket is partially consumed. So, even though 20 PC remained physically, the PAL constraint allowed only 5 PC to be confirmed — respecting the sequence and allocation rules.

If you want all customers to share remaining stock proportionally, you’d need to remove or adjust the sequence constraint or use “No Sequence Constraint” mode in PAL configuration.

entry.title: Re: Developing Mobile Apps with AI Agents: Introducing the MCP Server for mobile development kit
entry.content:
Hi @user017, 

This scenario is currently not supported. We do have integration of the MCP server in BAS on our roadmap.

Regards,
Ludo

entry.title: Re: No logs are found in secatt execution after exection...
entry.content: You have the possibility to look up the test execution logs in transaction: SECATT and also in the Launchpad - Test Suite - Automated Test Results. You can display the execution logs of automated test cases. For more information, see Test Execution Reports | SAP Help Portal. If any problem, please raise an incident under the component SV-SMG-TWB or SV-SMG-TWB-TCE for assistance.

entry.title: Re: SENARIO: OVER CONSUMPTION OF RAW MATERIAL IN SUBCONTR...
entry.content:
In a subcontracting PO:

  • The PO item is for the finished good (FG) and in your case, 10 MTR.
  • The components (raw materials) are managed through the component tab and linked via the BOM.
  • When you create a Subsequent Debit note referencing the PO, SAP uses the PO item’s unit of measure (MTR) because the reference is at the header/item level, not the component level.

So, even though you want to post a debit for 2 KG of raw material, the system defaults to MTR because that’s the PO item’s UoM.

This is standard SAP behavior — the subsequent debit note always inherits the PO item’s unit of measure. SAP doesn’t allow direct reference to component-level materials for debit/credit notes in subcontracting POs.

You have two main options:

OptionDescriptionImpact
Manual Subsequent Debit (no PO reference)Create a debit note manually for the vendor’s raw material (2 KG) without referencing the PO.It won’t appear in PO history but allows correct UoM (KG).
Custom BAPI logicUse BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST or BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CREATE with custom logic to reference the PO and override the UoM.You can force the debit note to appear in PO history while posting in KG.


For your case, since you need it to appear in PO history, you’ll likely need to:

  • use BAPI_INCOMINGINVOICE_CREATE
  • reference the PO item
  • pass the quantity and UoM (KG) explicitly in the ITEMDATA structure
  • ensure the GR-based IV indicator is set correctly

entry.title: Re: Requirement for BP Type-Based Customer Statement Emai...

entry.title: Facing this error while processing "HU is not in the work center. You cannot make changes"
entry.content: While Im testing on the Tile "Pack Handling Units Advanced," I have created a handling unit using the tab "Create HU," and then when I try to test the Tab "Repack HU," im getting the error "HU is not in the work center. You cannot make changes." Please give steps and what configuration i need to check.

entry.title: How to implement the approval of purchase orders and sales orders through interface calls
entry.content: We hope to implement the approval of purchase orders and sales orders in the SAP Public Cloud system through interface calls. Is there a corresponding API for the approval function of purchase orders and sales orders?

entry.title: Re: Does the WBS field on the sales order have an extension point?
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: Requirement for BP Type-Based Customer Statement Emai...
entry.content:
Hello @Ishrat ,

I performed a check and I think your request can not fully be accomplished, however you might be able to work with a workaround.

In the App Output Parameter Determination, you can add additional columns.

To do so,

  1. Choose your Rule “Customer Statement” and Determination Step “Receiver”
  2. Bring it into Edit Modus
  3. Choose Table Settings

 

  1. Choose “Insert Column / From Context Data Objects” either for Condition Columns or Result Columns
  2. Use “Search” to search for additional objects
  3. Pick and add via “OK”

 

For your specific context: I do not see Business Partner Type as an object. However, maybe one of the given objects like “Account Type” might be used as a workaround.

I hope this helps.

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

 

Kerisha Lawrence

Public Cloud | IAM, Procurement, Post Go-Live

https://www.linkedin.com/in/kerisha-lawrence

entry.title: Re: Check Tool (COC) - DuplicateCompetencyNameCheck
entry.content:
Hi Gelo,

Excuse me as I double read your description, the error is DuplicateCompetencyNameCheck.

The check tool will check each competency under all locales whether there are duplicated competencies or not. Please change the competencies to different names for all locales to solve this check tool error.

BR,
Yilin

entry.title: MR21 – New Price Field Greyed Out – Standard Price Not Updating for Materials (Plant 1701)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

We are facing an issue while attempting to update the standard price using transaction MR21 for the following materials in Plant 1701:

  • BI_DRYPIT_SKULL
  • COBBLES
  • MILLS_SCALE
  • MISS_ROLLHEAVY
  • MISS_ROLLLIGHT
  • PCM_SKULL
  • RUNNER_LADLE_SKULL
  • SPONGEIRON_FINES
  • TUNDISH_SKULL

Issue Details

  • While executing transaction MR21, the “New Price” field is greyed out.
  • Due to this, we are unable to update the standard price for the above materials.
  • This issue is occurring consistently for all the above materials.

System Impact

  • The issue is observed in the QAS (Quality) server.

Expected Behavior

entry.title: Re: Supplier DPR creation We are facing error _Account 65...
entry.content:
Hello,

I asked our internal chatbot for support and here's the response. Please check whether it helps you solve the issue:

  1. Automatic Account Determination:  Ensure that the GL account is correctly mapped in the configuration for special GL transactions. This can be done using the ‘Automatic Account Determination’ app (SSCUI ID: 100297). For more information, see 2716583 - No special G/L account defined error during down payment requests creation.

  2. Manage GL Account Master Data: Verify that the GL account is not blocked and is correctly set up in the ‘Manage GL Account Master Data’ app.
  3. Chart of Accounts: Ensure that the GL account is included in the chart of accounts YCOA.
  4. Please also check the following information: 3629494 - 'Account 65001000 does not exist in chart of accounts YCOA' error in Manage Automatic Payments App - S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

If you do not wish to use this GL account, you may need to adjust the configuration to use an alternative account that is properly defined in your test system.

#GenAI-Assisted Content

Kind regards,

Henrike

entry.title: Re: How to avoid 5-digit UoM limit using chained alternat...
entry.content:
Hello @JuanFrancisco_PayanMolina 

I'm afraid there is not a perfect solution to your issue. You can't really "chain" unit conversions - alternative unit of measure conversion is always defined to the base unit of measure.

The problem is described in the note 1607421 - Units of measurement larger than 99999 in material master (transaction MM01 / MM02). The resolution proposal is to:

Chose a unit of measurement which is larger than the current base unit of measurement.

But then you risk conversion rounding errors - note 122471 - Conversion of unit of measure for goods movements advises to use the smallest unit as a base unit of measure.

The notes are in fact contradictory. The root cause is conversion factor domain definitions limited to 5 digits. I don't think anyone will dare to change it.

You product must be quite small if you can fit a million or more pieces on a pallet. Do you really count them by pieces?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Include Header row in subsequent pages is not working for adobe header repetition issue

entry.title: Re: System is picking incorrect dep start date for ledger 3L in SAP public cloud
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 04 - Namespaces, contexts and name prefixes
IAM App & Successor in Same Business Role? Don’t Ignore This Warning (Part 1/2)
entry.content:
Quick Insight

If you see the warning:

“The assignment of an IAM app and its successor to the same business role is invalid. Please remove either the IAM app or the successor.”

👉   Don’t ignore it - it has real impact.

The Situation

As a Business Role Administrator, so far I assumed this message was just a harmless warning.

❌   No blocking error
❌   No immediate effect

👉   So I expected SAP to automatically handle deprecated apps over time.

What Actually Happens

With the deprecated app “Export Master Data” (F3251), I ran into an unexpected issue:

  • Added the successor business catalog
  • Followed SAP guidance

❌   But the successor app did not appear

Root Cause

The reason:

👉   Both the deprecated IAM app AND its successor were assigned to the same business role.

➡️   Result: Successor app was effectively hidden / not accessible

Solution

👉   Remove the old (deprecated) business catalogs or deactivate all deprecated apps within a business role

After cleanup:

  • Successor app becomes visible
  • Access works as expected

💡  Important Takeaway

This “orange warning” is misleading:

👉   It behaves more like a functional blocker than a simple remark

This is especially relevant with:

  • Upcoming release changes (e.g. 2608)
  • Increasing number of deprecated apps

Recommendation

1️⃣   Proactively review your business roles
2️⃣   Remove deprecated apps early (be smarter than I was=
3️⃣   Don’t rely on SAP to clean this up automatically

on’t rely on SAP to clean this up automatically

References

  • Successor MDG Apps Mandatory Soon | SAP Help Portal

Stay tuned for Part 2...

in which I will follow up with a step-by-step guide on how to fix the error message.

💬   Your Experience?

Have you seen similar issues with deprecated apps or missing successor apps?

Feel free to share - and if this saved you troubleshooting time, a like 👍 is always appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Worklist not seen in Process collection Worklist App
entry.content: Hello, thank you for your question. It has been forwarded to the experts. Kind regards, Henrike

entry.title: Re: System is picking incorrect dep start date for ledger...
entry.content:
Good day @S0027558245 

Thank you for your question.

The Public Cloud does not provide a separate depreciation key for ledger 3L that simply tells the system to use he posting date as the depreciation start date.

For asset depreciation, the key date is normally the asset value date, adjusted by the period control of the depreciation key.

See the following reference for more insights.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/9736dd51e2193946e10000000a44538d.html?locale=en-US

In simple terms:

  • The system does not normally use the posting date alone as the depreciation start date.
  • It uses the asset value date and the depreciation key period control.
  • If the asset uses depreciation to the day, the system always uses the asset value date as the depreciation start date, and period control is ignored. See KBA 3480268 for more details.
  • If posting date and asset value date are different, depreciation may start from the asset value date logic, not from the posting date.
  • You cannot create or freely adjust depreciation keys like in on premise.

See the following reference for details on predefined depreciation keys.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/3e5fcf2c768746049b5627bd5a42f720/a19cc2531bb9b44ce10000000a174cb4.html?locale=en-US

So, for your asset 50000007-0, the first thing you should check would be whether ledger 3L has a different depreciation area setting, asset value date, capitalisation date, or depreciation key behaviour compared with 0L and 2L.

Open Manage Fixed Assets, search for asset 50000007-0, go to valuation area for ledger 3L, compare the fields mentioned above

Open Depreciation key: Display details. Check the period control method for the 3L depreciation key you tested. If your requirement is to start depreciation from the exact posting date, ensure the asset value date of the acquisition posting is the same as the posting date.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

Markus

entry.title: Re: Next Phase of the New Table Build Experience: Switching Existing Stories
entry.content:
Hi @selti,

thanks for your question. When you are creating new stories in optimized design AND when you are migrating from classic to optimized, we will be using the new table experience automatically. 

During the migration from classic to optimized, the table will be migrated to the new table experience. When you create a new optimized story, the new table experience will be used automatically.

In both situatiions you should see a quick message in the builder panel confirming that you are indeed in the new table experience:

 

If you are observing different behavior, please let me know and create a ticket with SAP Support.

Best regards,
Anna

Regards,

M.Swaminathan.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 03 - Declarative field control with @mandatory
entry.content: 2f307500e74ca1453ccbb43d82dc456e751af9766c4c2511ad94824d444e6eb4

entry.title: Your S/4HANA & Ariba implementation is live, but your GRC ruleset do not have Ariba rules configured
entry.content:
Most SAP GRC implementations have a blind spot.

It is called Ariba.

And if your SoD ruleset doesn't account for it, you may be signing off clean access reviews on a system that's wide open to procurement fraud.

Across numerous SAP GRC implementations and Pre Go-Live and Post Go-Live reviews across the region, I typically get one of three responses when I ask whether the SoD ruleset covers cross-application risks between S/4HANA and Ariba:

  • "SAP GRC doesn't support integration with Ariba."
  • "We don't have IAG (Identity Access Governance)."
  • "We are planning to do it as part of Phase 2."

Let me translate those for you:

  • means "we haven't figured it out yet"
  • means "we know it's a gap but haven't prioritised it yet"
  • means "we are hoping the auditors don't notice before Phase 2"

That’s not a configuration gap. That’s your next audit finding !!!

The Integration Gap

1. GRC has no native Ariba connector.

Unlike the standard S/4HANA connector that pulls user assignments, role assignments, and authorisation objects, Ariba is opaque to GRC. There's no out-of-the-box integration. 

2. Ariba doesn't expose authorisations the way S/4HANA does.

There is no PFCG equivalent where you can drill into what a user can actually do in Ariba. No authorisation objects. No field-level permissions. Just system groups with pre-defined permissions.

3. In practice, this means a user could be registering fictitious suppliers in Ariba and processing invoices in S/4HANA and your GRC system would see nothing wrong.

So What Can You Do?

Here's the practical workaround I have implemented across multiple clients. The workaround is elegant in its simplicity where we bring Ariba into GRC's language by representing Ariba groups as custom Tcodes and template roles in S/4HANA.

To gain a better understanding of the SAP Ariba authorisation design, please review the list of access groups available in Ariba using the link below.

https://help.sap.com/docs/strategic-sourcing/strategic-sourcing-and-supplier-management-group-descriptions/strategic-sourcing-and-supplier-management-group-descriptions

SAP Ariba uses a group-based authorisation model where access is determined entirely by group membership.

Standard groups delivered in Ariba have fixed, pre-defined permissions that cannot be modified. While customers can create custom groups, these are essentially collections of standard groups (through group nesting) and do not allow individual permissions or actions to be customised, unlike PFCG roles.

Unlike PFCG roles, you cannot hand-pick permissions across Ariba groups. The permission boundary is always the standard groups and custom groups are simply collections of those standard groups.

For cross-application SoD analysis, the assessment should be performed between the user's assigned Ariba groups and their S/4HANA roles/access. As Ariba access is governed by group membership, the effective permissions granted by the Ariba groups should be evaluated together with the user's S/4HANA access to identify potential SoD conflicts.

Example

This is a standard SAP Ariba system group. If I want to create a custom group called "Supplier Onboarding Team Member (SUPP_ONB_TEAM_MEM)", I would create the custom group and assign the relevant system group as its parent group. The custom group would then inherit the permissions from that system group.

The Solution

Step 1: Create Custom Tcodes for Ariba System Groups. A sample is shown in the below screenshot. The Tcode descriptions can be written in simple, business-friendly language so that end users can easily understand the purpose of each group without requiring technical knowledge.

Step 2: Create template or dummy roles for ARIBA groups in S4HANA. Let us assume that you have an ARIBA group with name "Supplier Onboarding Team Member", you can create a PFCG role as per your own naming convention.

ARIBA GroupS/4HANA role nameDescription
Supplier Onboarding Team MemberAR:S_SUPPLIER_ONBOARDING_TEAMSee screenshot below

Maintain a comprehensive description of the role, including all actions that can be performed by users assigned to it in Ariba. This enables more effective access reviews, role design validation, and SoD risk assessments.

With the above approach, SAP Ariba groups are represented as template (dummy) roles in SAP S/4HANA, while Ariba system groups are represented as custom Tcodes in SAP S/4HANA.

Step 3:  This is the critical step. Without it, Steps 1 and 2 are just housekeeping. Mapping the Ariba Tcodes into your GRC ruleset is what transforms a workaround into a functioning control.  This will enable access risk analysis to identify potential Segregation of Duties (SoD) conflicts and critical access risks arising from Ariba groups. The custom Ariba Tcodes should be mapped to existing business functions and risk definitions within the Ruleset. New functions and risks may also be created to address Ariba-specific activities.

This approach ensures that Ariba access assignments, whether provisioned directly or through composite roles, are included in access risk analysis and remain subject to the client's governance, risk, and compliance controls.

Here is a real SoD risk that spans both systems and would be completely missed without this approach. Maintain a fictitious vendor and enter a Vendor invoice for automatic payment

  • Vendor Master Maintenance (ZPR01) + Process Vendor Invoices (ZAP02)

I have included the ARIBA Tcodes related to the supplier maintenance in this Vendor Master Maintenance function.

When a user who has access to process vendor invoices is being granted with the Supplier Onboarding Team Member (Ref S4HANA Role: AR:S_SUPPLIER_ONBOARDING_TEAM) group in ARIBA, the corresponding SOD risks will be highlighted as shown in the below screenshot.

The SoD risk didn't exist in S/4HANA alone. It didn't exist in Ariba alone. It existed in the combination of access across both systems and was  invisible to  GRC ruleset, invisible to most access reviews, and invisible to auditors who don't know to look for it.

That's the gap this approach closes.

Your GRC ruleset should reflect the reality of how your users actually work across applications and not within silos. If Ariba is part of your procurement landscape and those functions are not in your ruleset, your SoD analysis is not complete.

Don't let your blind spot become your audit finding.

Extending the GRC Ruleset — What Else Can You Define?

The supplier onboarding example above is just one of many cross-system SoD conflicts that become visible once Ariba is brought into your GRC ruleset.

Using the same custom Tcodes approach, you can define risks across the full procurement lifecycle. Here are some examples:

ARIBA AccessS/4HANA AccessPotential Risk Scenario
Register / onboard suppliersExecute payment runFictitious vendor fraud
Electronically sign contractsProcess vendor invoicesContract approval + Invoice processing by same person
Create sourcing projects & requisitionsPost goods receiptsFull P2P cycle without segregation
Mass update contractsMaintain purchase ordersBulk reprice contracts then self-raise POs

The pattern is consistent i.e., the fraud doesn't live in one system. It lives in the combination.

Once your Ariba groups are represented as custom Tcodes in GRC, any of these risks can be configured, monitored and reported just like any native S/4HANA SoD conflict.

Thank you for reading. I hope this gives you a practical starting point for bridging the Ariba–S/4HANA SoD coverage gap in your GRC implementation.

If you have questions, have implemented something similar, or want to share your own approach, please drop a comment below.

You can also reach me directly at Maddysmail26@gmail.com whether it's a quick question, a deeper implementation challenge, or just a conversation about SAP.

Let's continue learning, sharing and building on each other's experience 🙂

entry.title: Re: Unable to Update Sales Order Item Pricing Condition v...
entry.content:
Hi @Lakshmipathi - thank you for your response.

A quick follow-up question: You mentioned the Extensibility Explorer. As far as I know, the Extensibility Explorer has been retired. Are you referring to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub instead?

The reason I am asking is that the Sales Order (A2X) API in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub explicitly provides a PATCH operation for the Item Pricing Element entity (A_SalesOrderItemPrElement).

SAP Business Accelerator Hub

In my scenario, I am not trying to update a standard pricing condition but a custom condition type (ZPR0).

The PATCH request is executed successfully without any error, but the value is not updated in the sales order.

Kind Regards

Kathi

entry.title: Alternative Standard Mobile Application for Batch-Managed Outbound Deliveries Without EWM
entry.content:
Hello,

In our customer's system, warehouse operators are expected to perform goods issue processes using handheld barcode scanning devices in production sites where SAP EWM will not be implemented.

We evaluated the Pick Outbound Delivery application for this purpose. However, when an outbound delivery contains batch-managed materials, the application displays the following error message:

LS_SHP022
"Delivery contains items with batches and cannot be maintained."

Therefore, we are unable to recommend this application for our scenario.

We would appreciate your guidance on the following questions:

Is there any configuration, activation, or recommended approach that would allow the Pick Outbound Delivery application to be used with batch-managed materials?
If this is a standard product limitation, which standard SAP application is recommended in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud without EWM for warehouse operators using handheld devices to perform the following activities?

  • Select or scan an outbound delivery
  • Select or confirm the batch
  • Confirm the picked quantity
  • Post Goods Issue (PGI)

entry.title: Unable to Configure Activities for "Add Development Goal" Event in SAP SuccessFactors ISC
entry.content:
Product Area:
SAP SuccessFactors Intelligent Services Center (ISC)

Hi Experts,

We are currently working on a business requirement in SAP SuccessFactors where managers should receive a notification/email whenever an employee adds a development goal.

While exploring SAP SuccessFactors Intelligent Services Center (ISC), we identified the "Add Development Goal" event as a potential trigger. However, when we access this event in ISC, the following message is displayed:

"There are no activities enabled for this flow. Add activities from the activity library."

The issue is that we are unable to create or add any activities from the Activity Library for this flow. As a result, we cannot configure notifications or email actions related to the "Add Development Goal" event.

Our Objective:

  • Trigger a notification or email to the employee's manager whenever a development goal is added.
  • Use ISC event-based processing rather than custom solutions, if possible.

What We Have Observed:

  • The "Add Development Goal" flow is visible in ISC.
  • No activities are currently available for the flow.
  • We do not see an option to create or enable activities for this event.
  • Therefore, notifications cannot be configured for this scenario.

Questions:

  1. Is the "Add Development Goal" ISC event intended to support activities and notifications?
  2. Are there any prerequisites or configuration steps required to enable activities for this flow?
  3. Has anyone successfully configured manager notifications when employees add development goals?
  4. Is there an alternative recommended approach (Business Rules, Workflow, Alert & Notification Framework, etc.) to achieve this requirement?

Any guidance, documentation references, or implementation experiences would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

entry.title: Re: Plant language change from EN to JA ( Japanese) for JP00
entry.content:
Hi Manoj_Gheewala,

  1. Go to the SAP Easy Access screen.
  2. Navigate to System → User Profile → Own Data.
  3. Open the Defaults tab (or Address tab depending on the SAP release/version).
  4. In the Logon Language field, change the language from EN to JA.
  5. Save the changes and log on again for the language change to take effect.

Or using transaction code:

  1. Execute transaction SU3.
  2. In the Defaults tab, change Logon Language from EN to JA, save the changes, and log on again.

Best regards,

Thu Mai

entry.title: Re: Having issue with the storage locations for plant XXXX
entry.content:
Hi Challa,

I noticed that ServiceNow ticket 697195/2026 has already been created for this issue. Please continue the discussion through that ticket, as it is currently being handled through the support process and will be the appropriate channel for updates and resolution.

Best regards,

Thu Mai

entry.title: I would like to write on SCM630, but cannot find any courses on SCM601
entry.content:
Hello,

I would like to learn and get certification on Warehouse Management. Upon searching I found that SCM630 was the best fit at the most, but requires SCM601 as a prerequisite. My issue is that I cannot find any courses for it. Is there a way to write for SCM630 without SCM601?

I did see that there's EWM now, but I had that it is vastly different from WM. Would it be recommendable to do EWM even if my organisation has not reached EWM level?

entry.title: 0.01 Price difference in 34 depre are
entry.content:
Hello teacher

 

We have a price difference in 34 depreaciation area.

 

We ran KO88 to settlement inverstment order 532020 to Fix assets.

 

The Net book value of 01 book depreciation and 30 group depreciation are all settled to  zero,

 

But we still have 0.01 price difference in 34 depreciation area.

 

Could you please support us?How to fix this problem?

 

I try to use T-code ABAB and ABMA,but I can't solve this problem.Because 34 depreciation is generated automatically,I can't do any manual postings.

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 06 - Project specific configuration
Re: Managing JMS Queues Programmatically with the new SAP Cloud Integration JMS OData API
entry.content: Hi @MarcoKoch , why is this not documented at https://api.sap.com/package/CloudIntegrationAPI/odata, especially regarding the new API policy?

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Authorization Concept / Strategy
entry.content:
Hello @Wayne_Noble 

 

SAP Fiori Authorization Concept (Core)

https://help.sap.com/docs/FIORI_IMPLEMENTATION_740/79e3aabeb90d45299fbad56d5e7911a5/f8968e5476e0f557e10000000a423f68.html


User Management & Authorization (S/4HANA Fiori)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/7b24a64d9d0941bda1afa753263d9e39/20bc5d521178f55fe10000000a423f68.html?locale=en-US

SAP Fiori Security Overview

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/22bbe89ef68b4d0e98d05f0d56a7f6c8/9745802595654b86849074b0fd9bd51d.html

Creating Roles for Catalogs (Important for PFCG)

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIORI_OVERVIEW/9b4ee51ce91e4a9cadd5e6d43c2992ba/6f0e415370107d77e10000000a441470.html

 

Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: SAP Integration Suite - Connecting to S/4HANA Private Cloud OData with OAuth (Client Credentials
entry.content:
Hi @JaiSreenivasan ,

thank you for your question. The list below provides an overview of the authentication and SSO mechanisms that you can use for authentication at HTTP transport level for providing and consuming Web services on SAP Netweaver Application Server:

  • Standard Authentication (User ID and Password)
  • X.509 Certificates
  • Logon Tickets

As you can see OAuth is not supported for web services. 

Further details are available through this link. 

BR

Frederick

entry.title: Re: Supporting data migration from other systems (SAP Solution Manager, etc.)
entry.content: Hi Richard, thank you for reaching out. Synchronization of attributes is one of our next roadmap items, it is still not implemented.

entry.title: Re: Update and deletion processing of the final receipt f...
entry.content:
Hello @OliverGraeff 


CO13 reversal does not reset Final Issue because SAP treats it as a status flag, not a quantity-based field.


Thanks,
Sravan

entry.title: Re: Introducing Joule on Hands-on Practice Sandbox
entry.content: hello @Robert_17 , the issue is already under investigation. I hope I can update you soon
